there is a real number $ x $ greater than 1 that is not an integer, and satisfies the inequality:

$$
\{x\}+\left\{x^{3}\right\}+\left\{x^{5}\right\}+\cdots+\left\{x^{99}\right\}<\frac{1}{2^{99}}
$$

where $ \{x\} $ denotes the fractional part of $ x $. the fractional part of a number is the part of the number after the decimal point, and it is always between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive). for example, if $ x = 2.5 $, then $ \{x\} = 0.5 $, and if $ x = 3.14 $, then $ \{x\} = 0.14 $.
